name: El Hornillo R11 MTB Route
name_original: Montes del Bajo Tiétar y refugio Collado de la Casa – circular desde Hornillo por Parque de la Sierra de Gredos
description: The El Hornillo R11 MTB Route is a difficult 12.5-mile mountain biking trail in Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, with 2337 feet of ga
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Hornillo are primarily found within the Sierra de Gredos mountains, offering diverse terrain for riders. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, pine forests, and views of the Tiétar Valley. Riders can expect a mix of challenging ascents and "flowy" downhill sections, particularly in areas recognized for Enduro mountain biking. This landscape provides a robust environment for various mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around El Hornillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Mountain biking through the Sierra de Gredos, a 25.6 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the rugged terrain of the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Arenas, El Hornillo y Guisando — Sierra de Gredos, a difficult 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path. This route offers expansive views across the Tiétar Valley and through mountain landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Del Arenal a Guisando — Sierra de Gredos, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through pine forests and mountainous areas, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around El Hornillo is defined by the Sierra de Gredos mountains, pine forests, and the Tiétar Valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain routes. The routes in El Hornillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Hornillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Hornillo?
A: El Hornillo, primarily known for its trails in the Sierra de Gredos mountains, offers over 60 mountain bike routes. These range from easy paths to very challenging mountain routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Hornillo?
A: The terrain around El Hornillo is diverse, characterized by significant elevation changes, dense pine forests, and expansive views of the Tiétar Valley. Riders can expect a mix of challenging ascents and 'flowy' downhill sections, especially in areas known for Enduro mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Hornillo?
A: While many routes in El Hornillo are rated as moderate to difficult, there are a few easier paths available. Out of over 60 trails, 3 are specifically categorized as easy, providing options for those new to mountain biking or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in El Hornillo?
A: Yes, El Hornillo is well-regarded for its challenging mountain bike routes. The area features 27 difficult trails, with significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, particularly within the Sierra de Gredos. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494755869/mountain-biking-through-the-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountain biking through the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; offer demanding rides for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Hornillo?
A: Yes, you can find circular mountain bike routes in El Hornillo. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079387474/bajo-tietar-circular-desde-el-arenal-por-parque-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bajo Tiétar – circular desde El Arenal por Parque de la Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.0-mile (38.6 km) loop that takes you through the stunning landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in El Hornillo offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You'll traverse pine forests and enjoy panoramic views of the Tiétar Valley. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, and you might even pass mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/540292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Candeleda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is El Hornillo a good destination for Enduro mountain biking?
A: Yes, El Hornillo in La Adrada, Ávila, is specifically recognized for its Enduro mountain biking trails. The area has even hosted stages of the Open de España de Enduro, indicating its quality for the sport with 'flowy' downhill sections through pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Hornillo?
A: The Sierra de Gredos region, where El Hornillo is located, offers varied conditions. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the presence of pine forests and significant elevation suggests spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region around El Hornillo offers options for refreshments and accommodation. You can find local refuges and shelters, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elola Refuge – Laguna Grande de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Victory Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as places to rest or dine after a day on the trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Hornillo?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Hornillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging routes, stunning views, and the diverse landscape of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in the El Hornillo area?
A: While the primary mountain biking focus in El Hornillo (Ávila) is on the Sierra de Gredos, other locations named El Hornillo in Spain are known for waterfalls. For example, the Cascada del Hornillo near San Lorenzo de El Escorial and Los Hornillos Waterfall in Córdoba offer natural environments with waterfalls, though these are distinct from the main mountain biking area in Ávila.
tips: Beautiful section of single track through the mountains of Bajo Tiétar. 1km away is the Domingo Fernando Shelter next to a pool that will be great for hot summer days.
Shelter in the middle of a forest with a door and a fireplace. There is a fountain next to it, but in February it was dry. There was stagnant water that could be drunk if treated properly.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, making it suitable for experienced mountain bikers with a good level of physical fitness and endurance. It features significant elevation changes over its 20-kilometer distance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route takes approximately 2 hours and 24 minutes. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route is a circular trail, meaning you will finish at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near El Hornillo, within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos. Specific parking information is best found by checking local maps or community forums closer to your visit, as designated parking areas can change.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including sections of the Singletrack Trails of Bajo Tiétar. While the komoot data indicates singletrack, the broader region often includes tracks and secondary roads, so be prepared for a mix of surfaces suitable for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always check specific park rules before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot and dry in the Sierra de Gredos, and winters may bring snow or challenging conditions at higher elevations. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos for activities like mountain biking. However, certain areas or specific activities might require permits, especially during peak seasons or for organized events. It's always best to check the official park website for the most current regulations before your trip.

faqs.question: What points of interest or shelters are along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes by the Collado de la Casa y Fuente Shelter, which can be a useful spot for a break. Additionally, you'll experience the Singletrack Trails of Bajo Tiétar, offering scenic views and engaging riding.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route incorporates sections of other trails. Notably, it fully covers the 'El Hornillo' trail and also includes parts of '[CIMA AV17] Refugio de Mingo Fernando', 'El Arenal', 'Senda del Puerto del Peón', 'Valle del Tiétar', and '[CIMA AV11] La Centenera'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a ride on the El Hornillo R11 MTB Route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and potential for remote sections, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate navigation tools. Sun protection is also highly recommended, especially during warmer months.
